title: Array Index Out of Bounds Bug Fix: Find Max Pair Sum

statement.markdown:
Given an array of integers, find the maximum sum of a pair of elements. However, there's a bug in the code that causes it to consider indices outside the array bounds. Fix the code to make it work correctly.

Example input: 1
2
3
Output: 7 (max sum of pair 3 and 4)
Note: Array indices are 0-based.
statement.inputFormat:
First line: size of array
Next lines: space-separated elements of the array
statement.outputFormat: Maximum sum of a pair of elements
statement.notes: Input size is guaranteed to be between 1 and 10000

hints.text: Consider using a loop to iterate over the array

editorial.title: Find Max Pair Sum
editorial.intuition: The idea is to iterate over the array, keeping track of the maximum pair sum seen so far.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force: try all possible pairs, keep track of maximum sum
editorial.betterSolution: Use a dynamic programming approach to keep track of maximum pair sum
editorial.optimalSolution: Iterate over the array, for each element, update the maximum pair sum if needed
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution is O(n) time complexity and O(1) space complexity
editorial.dryRun: For input [1, 3, 5], maximum pair sum is 8 (1+7 or 3+5)

buggyStarterCode.python:
def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> int:

	n = len(nums)
	for i in range(len(nums) + 1):

		# maxSum = max(maxSum, num)
	return maxSum
